chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Principles of Data Science. (3-0). Credit 3. Theoretical foundations, algorithms and methods of deriving valuable insights from data; includes foundations in managing and analyzing data at scale, e.g. big data; data mining techniques and algorithms; exploratory data analysis; statistical methods and models; data visualization. Prerequisites: STAT 211 or ECEN 303; STAT 212 or CSCE 222; MATH 304. Cross-listed with STAT 335.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content:
Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): STAT 211 or ECEN 303; STAT 212 or
CSCE 222/ECEN 222; MATH 304. Cross Listing: STAT 335/CSCE 320.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

• Collect data from the web, manipulate and process large datasets, and conduct
exploratory data analysis.
• Apply and interpret basic exploratory statistical techniques including cluster
analysis and dimensionality reduction.
• Apply and interpret linear models to a variety of dataset types.
• Define and explain the key concepts and models relevant to data science, including
data cleaning and integration, data-intensive distributed computing, data mining
algorithms, and data visualization.
• Design, implement, and evaluate the core algorithms underlying an end-to-end data
science workflow, including the experimental design, data collection, mining,
analysis, and presentation of information derived from large datasets.
• Apply "best practices" in data science, including facility with modern tools.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Your final grade will be based on completion of the following requirements, following the
grading scale shown in Table 1.

• Weekly in-class quizzes. Quiz will be available at the start of the first class of
each week. Your overall quiz grade (QG) will be the statistical mean of the top 75%
of the individual quiz grades, i.e., the lowest 25% of your quiz grades do not count,
regardless of whether you completed them or not. This gives you a bit of flexibility:
a few low quiz grades may not affect your grade, and you may miss a few
unexcused quizzes (e.g., you arrived late to class, your battery died, connection
issues).
• Missed quizzes with an approved excused absence (see Excused absences)
will not be counted towards the total. For example, if we have 17 quizzes
during the semester, and you had 3 approved excused absences (e.g.,
documented illness), your grade will be based on the remaining 14 quizzes
following the procedure discussed above.
• Missed quizzes without an approved excused absence (see Excused
absences) will be graded as a zero and will be counted towards the total.
• Occasionally, you will be asked to complete a pop quiz about contents
covered in class since the previous quiz. This encourages students to take
notes during the lecture, pay attention and participate in discussions. Pop
quizzes will be treated as regular weekly quizzes when computing the overall
quiz grade.
• Please bring your Aggie ID card to take quizzes or participate on any class
activity. We do not accept any other form of identification.
• Homework assignments. There will be 4 homework assignments throughout the
semester, roughly every 2-3 weeks. Homework assignments must be completed
individually. To give you some degree of fault tolerance, your overall homework
grade (HG) will be the weighted average of the four grades: 40% of the highest
grade, 30% of the second highest grade, 20% of the third highest grade and 10%
of the lowest grade. For example, if you score 50 on HW1, 90 on HW2, 70 on HW3
and 100 on HW4, your homework grade is 86 (0.1 50 + 0.3 90 + 0.2 70 + 0.4 100),
which is higher than the unweighted mean of 77.5.
• Late submissions: Submissions received within 24 hours after the Canvas
deadline will receive an automatic penalty of 10 points. Submissions received
after this period will not be considered and will be graded as a zero.
• Code: homework submissions must include (1) a well-written report following
the submission guidelines in the assignment, and (2) code that is ready to be
executed. Submissions without code or without the report will be graded as a
zero. If your code does not run (for whatever reason), you will receive an
automatic penalty of 10 points but will have one opportunity to make minor
corrections to the code by requesting an appointment with the TA.
• Review period: Once homework grades are posted on Canvas, students have
24 hours to request a review of the grades via the official course email
address, with a clear explanation of the request (i.e., no fishing, please).
Grade revisions will NOT be considered after this period.
• Classroom etiquette. Classroom etiquette will follow the guidelines of Library Use
& Conduct, including: no talking, no cell phone use, and silencing of all electronic
devices. Strictly_prohibited is the use of (1) cell phones, unless there is an
emergency, and (2) audio devices (e.g., ear buds, headphones), unless there are
accommodation requests (e.g., hearing aids). Personal computers (e.g., laptops,
tablets) should always remain closed unless when used for class activities (e.g.,
taking notes, quizzes). Each individual violation of classroom etiquette will
result in a 1-point Etiquette Penalty (EP) on the FINAL course grade.

Your total points (TP) will be the weighted average of QG and HG, weighted in favor of
the lowest of the two grades, minus penalty points due to violations of classroom
etiquette:

Equation 1
TP=0.60×min(QC,HG)+0.40×max(QC,HG)-sum(EP)

For example, a student receives a quiz grade of QG=60 and a homework grade of
HG=100
and
three
EPs.
Thus,
their
total
points TP=0.60×min(60,100)+0.40×max(60,100)-3=0.60×60+0.40×100-3=73 or a C letter
grade according to Table 1 below.

chunks.title: Table 1. Grading scale.
chunks.content:
| Total points | Letter grade |
|---|---|
| 90 | A |
| 80 | B |
| 70 | C |
| 60 | D |
| Otherwise | F |

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not
considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Please refer to late submission policy under Homework assignments above.

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
A tentative schedule for the course, including assignments and due dates for submission
of course materials is available as a spreadsheet. Please refer to it regularly as it may
change during the semester based on eventualities.

chunks.title: Additional Course Information
chunks.content:
If you would like to request an excused absence, please follow these steps:

• Go to Student Rule 7 to determine if your situation is covered, then
• Send an email to csce-320-rgo@tamu.edu with:
• A description of your situation, and
• Which provision within Student Rule 7 applies
• Supporting documentation (doctor's note, cover letter, etc.)
• Your request will be reviewed, and you will be notified of its approval/rejection via
email

Timeline for submitting excused absences: Per Student Rule 7, you must make the
request prior to the day of absence. If this is not possible, you must provide notification
by the end of the second business day after the last date of the absence, including an
explanation of why advance notice could not be sent. Requests for excused absences
that do not meet these guidelines will not be considered.

chunks.title: PLAGLIARISM AND FABRICATION
chunks.content:
Homework reports containing plagiarized content (including self-plagiarism or Al-
generated) will be submitted to the Aggie Honors Office, with the recommendation that
the student receives an F* for the course. Please review the TAMU guidelines for
Academic Integrity and Plagiarism.

Occasionally, we find evidence of plagiarism after the homework reports have been
graded. For example, a prior report may be flagged when a new detector for Al-
generated materials becomes available. For these reasons, you should treat your
homework grades as provisional. Any prior submission that is later found to constitute a
violation will be submitted to the Aggie Honors Office, with the same recommendation
that the student receives an F*.

Any information (e.g., a doctor's note for an excused absences) that is found to have
been fabricated, falsified and/or misused will be submitted to the Aggie Honors Office
with the recommendation the student receives an F* for the course.

chunks.title: AI Statement
chunks.content: The use of Al is strictly prohibited. Please refer to Additional Course Information above.
